title: nepeta paucifolia
intro: Nepeta paucifolia, also known as Himalayan catmint, is a species of flowering plant in the mint family, native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Nepeta paucifolia include its ability to reduce anxiety and stress, promote sleep, and alleviate digestive issues. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
